issue.title: Admin Access should migrate the legacy browser secret key

coreClaim: Refactor web shell reads and migrates the legacy marinara_admin_secret Admin Access localStorage value to the current marinara-admin-secret key.

reproduction.method: Static source repro plus scratch helper proof

verification.baselineEvidence: pnpm check passed after Bunny storage-mutation fix

edgeCases.0: Legacy-only localStorage value is trimmed, returned, migrated to marinara-admin-secret, and legacy key removed
edgeCases.1: Current key wins when both current and legacy keys exist; stale legacy key is removed
edgeCases.2: Saving a nonblank Admin Access value trims it, writes the current key, and removes the legacy key
edgeCases.3: Saving blank Admin Access clears both current and legacy keys
edgeCases.5: Current-key read survives failed legacy cleanup removeItem
edgeCases.6: Legacy-only read returns the secret even if migration setItem throws

notes.0: Issue assigned to cha1latte before edits
notes.1: No local 2063 branch/worktree existed before worktree creation
notes.3: Rebased cleanly onto current origin/refactor before push
notes.4: PR body marks Feature Discoverability as N/A with reason for PR-aware discovery CI
notes.5: Addressed Bunny finding: legacy read can fail on migration write

riskClaimMatrix.coreClaim: Refactor web shell reads and migrates legacy Admin Access browser storage without requiring users to re-enter the secret.

entrypoints.0: Settings Advanced Admin Access field initialization/save/clear

positiveRowsTested.0: legacy-only value migrates and is read
positiveRowsTested.1: current+legacy prefers current and cleans legacy
positiveRowsTested.2: save writes current key and removes legacy

groundTruthFacts.0: origin/refactor had only marinara-admin-secret in SettingsPanel and remote-runtime

riskClaimMatrix.userActionCopy: No user action required when a legacy browser secret exists; clearing Admin Access clears both browser keys.
